The beach at Huntington Beach in Southern California was transformed into a white canvas after a fast-moving storm dumped at least an inch of pea-sized hail on the sand. The storm also brought lightning and rain, but it didn't stop surfers from hitting the shore. Some beach-goers even built snowmen on the coastline. The National Weather Service says this is the second winter storm to hit the area in several days. The storm was unusual, with only three other times in 34 years that the sand had turned white due to hail. After the storm, the cloud lifted to reveal blue skies and rainbows were spotted in Orange County.

The Aerial Bold project is a crowdsourcing tool that lets people find letters, numbers, and characters in satellite images. The project was set up by German designer Benedikt Groß and geographer Joey Lee. They raised $11,492 on Kickstarter and have since set up the Aerial Bold crowdsourcing tool. The tool lets people scour Mapbox images to find letters, numbers, and characters in the landscape. The project aims to develop a new font and software that can automatically find words and letters on images. The NHS has spent nearly £350,000 on removing tattoos for people who changed their minds about their body art. Over 2,000 people have received free laser treatment in the past four years. The NHS only provides tattoo removal if it is necessary for health reasons, such as significant distress or mental health problems. However, the true cost is likely to be higher as many trusts did not provide breakdowns of the reasons for laser treatments. The popularity of tattoos has increased due to celebrity fans, but four in ten people regret having a tattoo and one in six want them removed.
